6125286:
Devil's Nursery

The slums of Devil's Nursery, located in the Redroof neighborhood of Kintargo, are named for the large proportion of its residents who are cambions, most of whom fled here from less tolerant corners of Cheliax.
16070936:
Xin-Grafar

Xin-Grafar, the City of Golden Death, is the lost underground treasure city of Tar-Baphon, the Whispering Tyrant, located on the Isle of Terror.

28595286:
Mercerene Manor

Mercerene Manor is the residence of House Mercerene, boasting extensive courtyards and gardens, and is where the retired Pathfinder Haimon Heuff resides. Adventurers of all kinds visit the manor, captivating its old lord with passionate tales of adventure that evoke memories of bygone days.
45921026:
Limehouse Theater

The Limehouse Theatre in Rego Scripa is run by the infamous director Robahl Nonon, who has made actors cry after his harsh critiques. The theatre is quite small, and dress rehearsals are held more often than actual performances, which is preferred by the locals who can boo and throw tomatoes during them.
47027566:
Muliwan

Muliwan (pronounced MULL-one) is one of only two permanent settlements in the nomadic nation of Hongal, in Tian Xia. It is positioned at the southern end of the nation, with the capital of Ordu-Aganhei at the northern end.
60973846:
Black Tower

The Black Tower of Urgir in the Hold of Belkzen is a school of Belkzen war alchemy located on the northwestern side of the city's fourth level, northeast of the Big Hole.
78930806:
Icestair

Icestair is the largest settlement in the Realm of the Mammoth Lords, wedged against the wall of ice that marks the edge of the Crown of the World.
88712306:
Bos-Phargrumm

Bos-Phargrumm is an ice castle perched high among the peaks of the Tusk Mountains northwest of the Ginji Mesa. It is the castle of Jarl Gnargorak, the self-proclaimed king of all frost giants.

The fortress is constantly built on top of the decaying older layers underneath, with the jarl and his most favored minions living in the newest, highest layers. Deep in the lower collapsed levels, many dangerous creatures make their home.
104189716:
Faldamont

Faldamont is a small town in the western foothills of the World's Edge Mountains in northern Taldor.
110535866:
Saventh-Yhi

The ruined city of Saventh-Yhi (pronounced saa-vinth-YHEE) is located in the southern Mwangi Jungle, east of the Ocota River, just north of the Bandu Hills. The city was founded more than 10,000 years ago during the Age of Legend by followers of the Azlanti hero Savith, who died while defeating the serpentfolk god Ydersius in the Darklands' tunnels below.
118415146:
Cedar Court

Cedar Court is less of a settlement in Druma, and more a small group of the oldest dryads' trees in the region. This fey court is one of the most reclusive, and hides somewhere deep in the Palakar region.
123503116:
Eto

Eto is the largest city of central Osirion, located just north of the mountain range known as the Pillars of the Sun. It sits at the crossing of numerous overland trade routes, including the major one between Sothis to the east and Shiman-Sekh to the west, and has seen its fortunes wax with the increase of foreign trade since Osirion's independence. In addition to benefiting from this increased trade, it has also become a launching pad for adventurers seeking to plunder the country's many ancient tombs.
125306936:
Cutlass Cove

Cutlass Cove is located a quarter of a mile off the east coast of Rego Sacero on a small peninsula west of Westcrown. It is a resting place used by smugglers and pirates and has a few buildings near the shore.
127047606:
Sunder Horn

Sunder Horn is a small town in the Sellen Hills of eastern Numeria that is home to the Kellid Sunder Horns tribe.
149561266:
Eel's Skull

The Eel's Skull is a disused fortress in the Shackles. It is situated on Nalt's Island located between Bag Island and Shark Island, standing at the edge of a sheer cliff on the northern expanse of the island's eastern shore.

At one time it was the fortress home of the notorious pirate Nalt Tarbrow. It is said that there are Ghol-Gan dungeons beneath the fortress, but Tarbrow destroyed the fortress in a fit of madness, and no entrance to any dungeon has been found in the ruins. Yet the lowest levels of Eel's Skull still hide an entrance to the ancient Ghol-Gani region of the Darklands known as the Sleepless Realm. The ruins are still visible from the ships passing and every sailor knows that wealth lies somewhere deep within the cursed castle's abandoned halls and caverns.

Only two adventurers are known to have emerged from the dread dungeons of Eel's Skull alive and both were terribly altered by the experience. No attempts restore either to full health or sanity. Their eyes remained blind and pupil-less, their skin remained ash white and they continued to tremor. One eventually killed himself by setting himself on fire (as Nalt's own fiery end), while the other, a Pathfinder named Gelrue Palane, is last rumored to have set off for Besmara's Throne.
151688926:
The Foundry

The Foundry is an ancient fortress constructed centuries ago in the eastern Kodar Mountains at the height of dwarven power on the surface of Golarion. Then called the Ironhearth Foundry, they dedicated it entirely to the pursuit of metallurgy, engineering, and smithing, taking advantage of the surplus of nearby mines. Like the Sky Citadel of Urgir, the Ironhearth Foundry eventually fell to the orc hordes in -3709 AR.
154425496:
Carpenden

Carpenden (sometimes spelled Carpendren) is the most influential city in the Carpenden region of Andoran. Located on the north-central Carpenden Plains, it is surrounded by rolling hills that lend themselves to the creation of numerous plantations and orchards.
180263976:
Laughing Gale

The Laughing Gale is one of many taverns in Greengold, though it stands out as the oldest and longest-running. It's a popular hangout, and many of the city's fights start after a drink here, sometimes spilling over into the rest of the city.
186339966:
Witchlight

Witchlight was a small settlement on the island of Devil's Elbow southwest of Riddleport. Founded by Yaris Neraken in 4680 AR, it was located on the island's highest point.
193260666:
Sea Wrack Chapel

The Sea Wrack Chapel is a small temple dedicated to Gozreh that serves the pirate city of Port Peril.

Like many seaside settlements, Port Peril has a small shrine dedicated to Gozreh for pious sailors to make offerings to the Lord of Waves. No larger or more impressive than the shrine of a typical fishing village, Sea Wrack Chapel is made from driftwood and discarded fishing nets but is still large enough to provide shelter to those seeking Gozreh's guidance.

Sea Wrack Chapel is tended to by Father Ignalus, a halfling priest who accepts Gozreh worshippers of any creed, including in the form of Shimye-Magalla, a dualistic deity worshipped in the Mwangi Expanse. Father Ignalus is also a meticulous record keeper and has kept track of every single shipwreck that has occurred in the vicinity of Port Peril.
196779526:
Ridwan

Situated in the foothills of the Menador Mountains, Ridwan sits on a hostile volcanic plain. The city is the religious center for the faithful of Zon-Kuthon, as it is said that the Midnight Lord first emerged upon Golarion from the smokey Shadowflame Rift that dominates the city's central square. The rift itself is believed to be a gateway to a particularly unpleasant chasm on the Netherworld known as the Deeping Darkness. Apart from being a religious center, Ridwan is also home to the training camps of Nidal's military, who hone their skills to spread the word of Zon-Kuthon through guile or force throughout the world.
207252266:
Citadel of the Alchemist

The fortress called the Citadel of the Alchemist is carved out of the base of a mountain in the Barrier Wall known as the Bent Razor in Thuvia. Above ground it appears as a modest stone structure. However, below ground there are vast tunnels housing alchemical traps and guardians. Hidden somewhere in this network are some of the best alchemical laboratories in the Inner Sea region. Artokus Kirran, creator of the sun orchid elixir, lives in the Citadel of the Alchemist, which was built for him many years ago.

Every year a blind, mute servant comes forth from the citadel with six vials of the elixir, to be auctioned off in one of Thuvia's five city-states.

The Stonewarden druids of the Barrier Wall mountains have a formal agreement with Thuvia to assist travelers that are seeking to reach the Citadel of the Alchemist.
231339486:
Thulsadus

Thulsadus is one of the many cities of the naga empire of Nagajor. Nagajor consists of 25 different kingdoms, each of them ruled over by their own naga monarch; the biggest cities of Nagajor each form the capital of a smaller naga kingdom.
233822106:
Alabastrine

Alabastrine is a city in Druma built in 4607 AR to accommodate Arodenite refugees after the deity's death. However, the migration never happened in the numbers its investors expected, and its hasty construction ceased before it was completed. High Prophet Kelldor purchased the partially finished city in 4711 AR, and since then the Druman government has auctioned control of the city's districts in five-year spans to the highest bidders. The most recent auction on record occurred in 4716 AR.
243124636:
Azurestone

The Galtan city of Azurestone takes its name from a the massive blue menhir that towers 100 feet above the town. Located on the plains north of the Boarwood, Azurestone has been relatively isolated from Galt's revolutionary fervour until recently. The stone is an object of religious reverence to the dwarves of the nearby Fog Peaks, who believe it to be a spear that was thrown down from Heaven in prehistoric times by their patron god Torag. These dwarves make regular pilgrimages to Azurestone, pumping up the local economy. Rumors of hidden chambers beneath the stone are beginning to attract adventurers and other undesirables to the city, who have also brought Galt's infamous revolutionary spirit with them.

A trade route links the city with nearby Edme. Azurestone's patron deity is Erastil, but most of the benign gods are honoured by the citizens. The locals are mostly farmers and miners. Ordinarily, the city's location would make it subject to harsh winters, but these are mitigated by the influence of a relic known as the Vernal Key.
251790936:
Galduria

Galduria is a town in central Varisia, located on the western shore of Ember Lake. Generally thought of as a dependent of the city-state of Magnimar, most of the town's wealth is generated by the trade of grain and lumber across the lake and along the Lampblack River. Its true claim to fame is its arcane college, the Twilight Academy, the founding of which in 4629 AR by Galdurin Barmier was the establishment of the town. The Twilight Academy rivals the larger colleges in Varisia, the Stone of the Seers in Magnimar, and Korvosa's Acadamae. The college was founded specifically to avoid the political pressure and intrigues of those two cities. The Twilight Academy has a reputation for undertaking many experimental and unconventional practices. Nevertheless, locals turn the other way regarding its strange ways in exchange for the money that the college is able to provide for various civic projects.

An

261691166:
Pug's Contraptions

Pug's Contraptions is a clockwork-creation business and tinker shop in the northwest of Magnimar's Ordellia district that generally deals in small contraptions such as toys, unique locks, and other baubles. It is run by old Morgamer Pug, a halfling who is in direct competition with the much-larger Golemworks.

Pug has carved himself out a small niche in only dealing in smaller, clockwork machines, but would love to cut into the Golemworks' business. To this end, he has an ongoing agreement with Venture-captain Sheila Heidmarch of the city's Pathfinder lodge to buy any construct her agents may come across in their journeys. The guildsign for the store is a pig whose mouth is stuffed with lockpicks, a remnant of the owner's first business (which he still practices when he can't sell clockworks) of creating locks and other small metalworks such as bird cages.

Grand Dance Hall of Kortos

286338716:
Qaharid

Qaharid, also called the Turquoise City, is a major mining city in the Maharev region of Qadira.
297136266:
Swiftrun

Swiftrun is a village of superstitious people in Isger, located on the Conerica River about 50 miles from Saringallow.
303813266:
Viridian Nexus

The Viridian Nexus is a grotto within the Verduran Forest. Corazal, a very old conrasu, took root in the area, creating the grotto to maintain the forest's balance. Several buildings made from Corazal's wood serve as shelters for their attendants.

Corazal can share their knowledge with those they deems worthy by showing a repository of events that occurred in the forest.
329401876:
Martel

The city of Martel is in the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar, along the Ustradi River west of Alkenstar City and east of the Gunworks. Its most notable features are the two enormous buildings that make up most of the city, which are connected by a number of stone and metal bridges. The Grand Duchy of Alkenstar maintains an extensive treasury in Martel, composed of vaults below the city that are guarded by both clockwork and explosive traps.

The Grand Duchy's gunmarshals make their headquarters in Martel. Additionally, the town is the center of banking for the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar, where the financial practices are as advanced as Alkenstar's level of technology. While this makes Alkenstar effective in their financial dealings, it has left the city-state with a slightly-less-than-trusted reputation throughout the Inner Sea region.
351746706:
Blackridge

Blackridge is the young capital of the Chelaxian region of Devil's Perch, within the Archduchy of Hellcoast.

357353036:
Battle of Falheart

The site of the Battle of Falheart is situated on the edge of the far eastern hills of Numeria, near the border with Brevoy. The forces of Prince Rathhorn and Sabline the Traitor met here in 3441 AR, but were all simultaneously struck dead by an unknown force the moment before they crashed into one another. The place is now rumored to be haunted and cursed, but is certainly inimical to all life, and no one dares approach it. The goddess of death, Pharasma, is said to personally claim this field, although her particular motivations in this are unknown.
359512406:
Broken Shield Castle

Broken Shield Castle is a small, isolated castle deep in the Mindspin Mountains of western Molthune, within its Umbral Basin province. Built at some point between 4714 AR and 4717 AR by Captain Breanth Aliskel after her retirement from the Imperial Army, it now hosts a small but growing force of bandits and mercenaries under her command.

373952696:
Larrad

Larrad is a large dwarven city in the region of Five Kings Mountains. The city is built up around a cluster of temple-caverns carved out by the dwarves in ancient times during the first period after the completion of their Quest for Sky. The temple-caverns served as a place of worship for their gods and as a necropolis for their honored dead. Over the centuries, the temples grew into great complexes hosting many dwarven clerics and acolytes. At the same time, the support settlements around the temples expanded and overlapped.

Larrad's necropolis is used by only the renowned members of dwarven aristocracy. The Cathedral of Magrim still maintains the tombs of the Five Kings' oldest residents, and employs necromantic magic to commune with the spirits of the ancestors. The Cathedral also supports expeditions into the ruins of Saggorak which is under threat from undead and creatures from the Darklands.

After the death of Gorum in 4724 AR, the dwarven deity Grundinnar and an array of dwarven heroes battled the primordial being Otolmens over the fate of a warshard that crashed at the gates of Larrad. According to the Molthuni novel When Gods Make War, Otolmens claimed the warshard was a threat to the physics of reality and demanded that Larrad and all beings exposed to the warshard must be deleted from existence, which Grundinnar refused to allow. As Grundinnar battled Otolmens, dwarven heroes rushed to Grundinnar's aid, were slain, and were raised to serve Otolmens as celestials. However, both divine entities and nearly all of the dwarven combatants were suddenly removed from reality in a loud flash, as if neither had ever existed.
376295906:
Eagle Garrison Fort

The Eagle Garrison Fort is a fortress of the Eagle Garrison located in the northern Kortos Mounts foothills, about 100 miles' travel from Diobel on land by way of Meravon.

Westcrown

393027336:
Sotto

Sotto is a tiny thorp in the Meratt County region of Taldor.
394048496:
Sable Sands

The Sable Sands are a desolate location in the southeastern deserts of Katapesh that is avoided by most travellers; the only people who visit are the occasional lost travellers who mistake it for a distant oasis.

The Sable Sands are about 100 feet in diameter and filled with a tarry, black substance that covers the ground in strange patterns. From the midst of the tar protrude several weather-worn tombstones, though no one knows who or what may have been buried beneath the sable sands.

The tar that permeates the ground seems to suck the happiness and positivity out of all who come near it, leeching the warmth from the very air itself. Though no undead have ever crawled from the tar-filled graves, the area does seem to attract them, drawing all sorts of living dead toward this desolate site.

398930886:
Old Forest Tower

The Old Forest Tower is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower is a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat 10-foot-wide top and four uneven tiers.

The tower is located in the Swardlands on the Isle of Kortos in the Immenwood.

Unlike the other Aeon Towers, this tower contains a shrine to Aroden inside.
407616376:
The Three Furies

The Three Furies is the name given to the three highest peaks of a mountain range in the center of the Isle of Terror. A storm of void energy surrounds the mountains, making it impossible to see the peaks from the base. However, if one passes through the clouds, the summits can be seen rising to great heights. From the top, one can observe the Wizard-King's Pit at the epicenter of the range, filled with the most horrific creatures on Golarion.
408676216:
Stormhollow

Stormhollow, formerly known as Lorra's Hollow, was a village located in the Umbral Basin that was destroyed by velstracs during a shadowstorm. The village was founded by halflings who escaped the empire of Cheliax and sought a more fulfilling life. Those hopes were shattered by the velstrac attack, which transformed the inhabitants into greater shadows that now roam the village. Their houses are now filled with spikes and nails, and the flora has vanished from the streets.
429305506:
Todorokaze

Todorokaze (pronounced toh-doh-roh-Kah-zeh) is one of the many cities that dot the tengu kingdom of Kwanlai. It is the only city in the Kimu Mountains and a throughpoint for travelers and caravans crossing the range, and also is home to Tian Xia's largest shrine to the tengu deity Hei Feng. Todorokaze is known for its volatile weather, which its structures harness through myriad use of lightning rods.

The thieves' guild of Todorokaze is responsible for the Autumn of Iron Tears, a conflict with the thieves' guild of the city of Seidoyaji that escalated into a full-blown proxy war between the two cities.
443474136:
Jacovo's Stables

Jacovo's Stables is a small ranch near town where good quality horses are sold or loaned. Its owner, Jacovo, is an honest man with a good attitude who is helped in his business by his nephew.

Shoreline Drinkhall

469916396:
Halgrim

The city of Halgrim is the second largest settlement in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings; only Kalsgard is bigger. Located on the northwestern shore of Battlewall, one of the largest islands in the Ironbound Archipelago, Halgrim was founded by King Igarven in 2655 AR. Today, it is ruled by the Linnorm King White Estrid from her palace. The Rustflow River flows out to the sea through Halgrim and, famously, seven bridges cross the river within the city. The city's defenses are greatly enhanced by the constant presence of White Astrid's linnorm thrall, Boiltongue.
471407356:
Ringsmith Ranch

Ringsmith Ranch is one of the Five Kings Mountains towns near the Andoran border that benefits from the region's caravan trade. Primarily dedicated to raising pack mules for transportation, Ringsmith Ranch is experiencing increasingly frequent bandit attacks.
478673706:
Shrine of Saint Senex

The Shrine of Saint Senex is not dedicated to a deity but instead to the centuries-dead Senex, an oracle who devoted her life to recovering the drowned dead of Nisroch Bay and the Yolubilis River. The shrine is now all but forgotten by the modern world; Saint Senex fell in popularity after the Chelaxian Civil War, lying unremembered on a stretch of swampy coast on Argo Isle, just south of the city of Kintargo. The driftwood shrine is devoted to drowned sailors, including a buried sanctum that is home to the bodies of dozens of unknown drowned. The shrine is shielded from magical divination and has sometimes been used as a secret safe house by Hellknights of the Order of the Torrent. Thanks to its decline in prominence, the shrine's only inhabitants are now a pair of rag-clad oracles.
499014446:
Cliffside Tavern

The Cliffside Tavern in the Castle Clarion district of Katapesh is perched at the edge of the cliffs overlooking the ocean. It is a popular tavern for locals and a romantic spot for couples. Azhmeer Murlawden is the owner of the tavern who often entertains his customers playing soft music on his dulcimer.

505873136:
Thrushmoor

The settlement of Thrushmoor can be found in the Ustalavic county of Versex. A fishing community on Lake Encarthan's Avalon Bay, the town is known to harbor a significant number of followers of the unsavory Old Cults, and the equally unsavoury Night Heralds.
509841496:
Djeneg

Djeneg is a village in the Footprints of Rovagug area of Osirion. It is located near the mineral spring known as The Great Pool. The Great Pool has been visited by Osirian nobles for millennia, and the village of Djeneg grew up around the pool to support its visitors.
511871786:
Korholm

Korholm is the gateway to Molthune, the seat of the so-called First General Lord, and the territorial seat of the Shrikewood province. Located at the mouth of the Nosam River, Korholm is also Molthune's largest port on Lake Encarthan. Soldiers and officers bound for the war against Nirmathas drill here. The city's entrance by land is the great Green Gate, which leads to a wide avenue that runs to the Hall of Governance at the city's center. Korholm is home to Molthune's most elite soldiers and its western third, the Arsenal District, houses those soldiers.

Ortalaca

553356266:
Creeping Watcher

The Creeping Watcher is a 70-foot-tall statue in the heart of the Vargas Swamp.

Though draped in vines and surrounded by dense trees, the Creeping Watcher resembles its sister statue, the Crouching Jackal. its body is sleek and cat-like, though covered in carved scales while its head is clearly reptilian. Unlike the Crouching Jackal, the Creeping Watcher is not carved from a single solid lump of stone, and its eyes are made of some sort of luminous yellow stone. Like its sister statue, the Creeping Watcher's origins are surrounded by mystery, though it is generally held that the Creeping Watcher is the more sinister of the two. Some even suppose that the Crouching Jackal serves as some sort of guardian keeping the Creeping Watcher in check. Whatever the truth is, the location has a bad reputation nowadays as it is believed to be the site of foul worship and evil rituals.
563533386:
Temple of Xanthuun

The waterlogged Temple of Xanthuun is located off the coast of the Eribastos Peninsula in the eastern Sodden Lands. It contains the legendary treasure hoard of Ammelon VI, last ruler of the pre-historic cyclops kingdom of Ghol-Gan.
564754996:
Sevenarches

Sevenarches is a town in the southwestern River Kingdoms built around seven stone arches scattered around the settlement, and a loosely organized nation of the surrounding wildlands. It is an ancient site sacred to elves, but has not been in elven hands since they left Golarion for the safety of Sovyrian millennia ago.

Since then, Sevenarches has been under the rule of the Oakstewards, a group of secretive druids. Indeed, Sevenarches is the only surviving kingdom from the initial wave of Kellid migration into the River Kingdoms region after the exit of the elves.

Pillar of Palamia

The Pillar of Palamia is an ancient obelisk made from basalt believed to be a monument to a forgotten god in the northwestern Whisperwood of central Cheliax. Others claim that it is a god's prison, and that the Pillar sticking into the ground is itself the nail holding in the trapped deity.
599958496:
Osprey Club

The Osprey Club, located in the Naos district of Magnimar, caters to a restrictive clientele of the richest families in the city. Gambling, drinking, and smoking are common activities here; there is also a brothel, for those so inclined. The exterior of the club is guarded by an iron fence to keep the onlookers outside. Green and gold magical lights illuminate the outside every night. The club is currently run by Kayasi Zivatchi.

606668006:
Yan Tlomíl

Yan Tlomíl is the largest agricultural centre in Xopatl since the fall of Chídanza. The land surrounding it yields an incredible bumper crop each year, but no one knows which crop will benefit, so each farmer plants multiple crops each year and waits to see which will flourish. When one of the plants starts thriving beyond all the others, it sparks a mad rush of farmers scrabbling to secure more seeds and land to grow more of this bumper crop. Due to this element of chance and the chaotic competition to capitalise on the flourishing crop, Yan Tlomíl's agricultural influence has yet to equal Chídanza's.
610794906:
Billivin's Benevolent Balms and Effulgent Elixirs

Billivin's Benevolent Balms and Effulgent Elixirs is a curious shop run by the eccentric Billiver Billivin, and is located in the southwest of the Ordellia district in the Varisian city of Magnimar. A magic shop of sorts, Billivin's is overflowing with every sort of potion, elixir, scroll, alchemical supply, ingredient, book, papyrus, piece of sculpture, spell component, and other bric-a-brac imaginable, due to the fact that its gnomish owner is a compulsive collector (some would say hoarder) whose organizational skills leave something to be desired.
617213496:
Dimcrater

Dimcrater is a mysterious crater located in the southernmost tip of Kyonin's Fierani Forest, well within the Tanglebriar, the domain of the nascent demon lord Treerazer. It was once known as Arabrecht, the manor home of the timeless and powerful Archmage Aelthian, prior to its disappearance during Aelthian's attempt to banish Treerazer in 2988 AR.

645708296:
Dome Hive

The Dome Hive is one of the cyclopean monuments of ancient Thassilon, built with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants. It is located in the modern Ironbound Archipelago, which was the Thassilonian nation of Edasseril.
647786586:
Billivin's Benevolent Balms and Effulgent Elixirs

2 locations:
  • Mistress of Angels
  • Red Kestrel Imports
649122386:
Zimar

One of the main defensive Taldan strongholds against any future invasion from the south, Zimar is vital to the long term survival of the nation. Even though the war with Qadira, the Grand Campaign, ended over 100 years ago, Zimar still serves as a bastion for the Taldan army and is home to several Taldan Phalanx regiments (the name given to one of their huge formations of heavy infantry) and several other formations including cavalry and capital ships. Even though Taldor has not been openly at war with its southern neighbor since 4079 AR, the city remains ever watchful and is the most heavily fortified settlement in Taldor.
659167936:
Drowned Eye

The Drowned Eye, an underwater pit about four miles west of Acisazi, has long been regarded by the aquatic elves of that village as a cursed place. Its founders placed protective wards around the pit, and generations of elves avoided the area. Ironically, the notorious site's reputation has helped protect the village from outsiders.

Fair Fortune Livery

677530686:
Undarin

The central city of Undarin in Sarkoris is located on the Sarkora River at its confluence with the dry bed of the Rolling River, and was once a center for the selling of livestock and ore in the kingdom of Sarkoris. Once the city was overrun by demons after the opening of the Worldwound in 4606 AR, its stockyards and animal pens were used to temporarily control the human population before being sent off for further processing. Like most of the cities in the region, the holy warriors of the Fifth Mendevian Crusade deemed Undarin to be too corrupted to be rebuilt, and instead razed the city in 4718 AR.
680244816:
Palace of Thirteen Spires

2 locations:
  • Scriveners' Square
  • Silk Castle
680984876:
Tashagrot

The ruins of Tashagrot are all that remains of the capital of the former Irriseni province of Rimetusk, wiped out by predators coming from the Realm of the Mammoth Lords that were eager to regain their old territories. It is said that the site, which lies on the eastern bank of the Gullik River south of Coldwater, is haunted by its massacred residents.
694670136:
Rangdol

Rangdol is a city of scholarship in southern Zi Ha. Its guilds—which include the Engineering & Architecture Guild, Mages Guild, and Scholars Guild—help to develop some of the nation's most prominent intellects, and the city is also renowned for its automated transportation, libraries, and second underground city that accommodates its population during the intense summers.
701290016:
Kaer Maga

710937936:
Tokanos

Across Thieron, the legacy of its minotaur builders endures in their stone monuments, which draw pilgrims and travelers from across Casmaron. Foremost among Thieron’s wonders is the cliffside complex of Tokanos. Here dwells Ilegia, an immortal medusa oracle whose prophecies draw a steady procession of blindfolded supplicants; nobles, merchants, priests, and powerbrokers alike. Her words carry undeniable weight, shaping decisions far beyond the temple’s windswept ledges. Some come seeking truth. Others come seeking advantage. But it is said those who attempt to sway her through flattery or bribery leave a more permanent offering. The halls of Tokanos are lined with statues; silent, lifelike reminders of those who believed prophecy could be bought, and discovered too late the cost of such presumption.
716494326:
Drenchport

The city of Drenchport is on Tempest Cay in the Shackles. Its proximity to the dreaded Eye of Abendego means the city is constantly lashed by storms. The city itself is constructed from driftwood and the remains of wrecked ships.

Drenchport's most infamous tavern is the Drowned Dwarf. It serves surprisingly tasty black kelp beer, but is notorious for its very high murder rate.

The ruler of Drenchport is the pirate lord known as the Master of the Gales.

The city is the home of the gnome salvager Tho Rimplethember, who is well known as an expert on the currents and winds of the dangerous Eye of Abendego.
723879266:
Stone of the Seers

1 locations:
  • Our Lady of Blessed Waters
738793666:
Kaddodi

Kaddodi is a ruined Ghol-Gan city that lies within the Kaava Lands region of the Mwangi Expanse just north of the Oubinga River. The brutal religious practices of the ancient Ghol-Gan civilization as it collapsed has made its ruins particularly attractive to the god-loathing asura. Kaddodi is home to one such asura, Parveen, who was imprisoned within the city by Jatembe's Ten Magic Warriors, but over the millennia the wards holding Parveen have weakened substantially.
742394666:
Grand Temple of the Peacock Spirit

The Grand Temple of the Peacock Spirit, headquarters of the cult of the Peacock Spirit, is located on an island in a lake within a hidden valley near the source of the first tributary of the Chavali River, deep in the Kodar Mountains.
742724946:
Stark Point

Stark Point is a trading post on the River of Lost Tears in the extreme east of Sargava. It is southeast of Fort Bandu and north of Kalabuto.

It was founded in 4414 AR as a mining community under the leadership of the church of Aroden. As a result, in addition to an inn, stable, and general store, it also boasts a surprisingly large cathedral. However, the mineral deposits were eventually exhausted, and the death of Aroden led to the cathedral being closed down and boarded up. Most of the population soon relocated to Kalabuto, and Stark Point become little more than a general store.

The abandoned cathedral might be haunted, and some have claimed to have seen strange lights and heard chanting coming from within.

In 4690 AR, the Sclerizi brothers—Arno, Carlo, Gudo, and Ikhaia—bought the store and converted it into the Korir Crossing Trading Post.
748851176:
Rimakak

Rimakak is a former Erutaki settlement on the coast of Lake Nallishoot in the Gaarjuk Hills in the Crown of the World. This village was cursed by a qallupilluk named Arnaalak, and the villagers, unable to fight back, fled the village.
750263746:
Logas

Logas is the second city of the nation of Isger. It was once the capital until that honor passed to Elidir, and its nobility dream of restoring Logas to its rightful status as the country's first city.
803811606:
St. Ilnea's Fountain

St. Ilnea's Fountain is a spring in a rock-ringed vale along the Menador Mountains, just west of the Chelaxian town of Taggun Hold in the Archduchy of Menador.
809490126:
Dhalanagar

Dhalanagar serves as the de facto capital of the relatively young mahajanapada of Nayapul in Vudra.
821784026:
Wreck of the Paradise

The Wreck of the Paradise lies forty feet underwater in the Claybottom Lake. The ship served as a casino for the mysterious Lucrecia, but in 4707 AR it sank in a fire, and the skeletons of its dozens of patrons at the time are scattered throughout the area.
823235176:
Dusk Market

6 locations:
  • Devildrome
  • Jacovo's Stables
  • Obrigan Gate
  • Pleatra

  • Verennie's Lair
833385136:
Licktoad Village

The Licktoad Village was where the Licktoad goblins of Brinestump Marsh lived in huts. The atmosphere was foul, and since it blocked a road, they harassed travelers who tried to pass through the area. The leader of the tribe was Rendwattle Gutwad, who didn't speak because his deep voice would terrify his subjects.
845658056:
Mama Shrog's Solutions

Mama Shrog's Solutions is a shop that was bought years ago by a woman called Mama Shrog. The building has a grotesque history, having belonged to a painter who murdered his entire family to obtain the specific colour of red he sought. Mama Shrog is a curious old woman who claims to be able to answer her visitors' questions, and although she has no children, she calls everyone her own. The shop primarily sells alchemical products to address her customers' physical ailments, and although people view Shrog with suspicion because she doesn't hide her faith in Lamashtu, there is no evidence that she has committed any crimes. Several champions and holy warriors have tried to expel her from the city and close the shop, but they haven't made any progress through legal means. When some Firebrands have tried to forcibly remove her, demons appear to defend her with extreme violence, which she justifies as self-defense when questioned by the guards. Although the shop is the only obvious temple of the goddess, shrines are also springing up throughout the city, taking advantage of Absalom's religious freedom.
847862096:
Zolurket Mines

The Zolurket Mines, once called Tar-Urkatha, are played-out platinum mines. Zolurket is a Kelish word for "Dark Death".

Zolurket remains abandoned for two reasons: the lack of worthwhile ores and minerals, and the presence of undead predators. The types of undead that lurk in the mines are unknown. There are rumors that Zolurket was abandoned before its wealth was depleted, and a mining company based in the city of Katapesh is attempting to reopen them.
860406736:
Wyrmwatch

Both a functioning lighthouse and a tribute to Magnimar's founder Alcaydian Indros the Wyrmwatch, or Wyrmwatch Tower, is a one of the city's most prominent landmarks. The Wyrmwatch sits at the western most edge of Outcast's Cove at the very tip of Beacon's Point jutting out into the Varisian Gulf warning ships away from the dangerous spur of rock and welcomes them to the busy harbour of the Dockway and the Bazaar of Sails.

It supposedly sits upon the very site where Alcaydian Indros slew the mighty Sea Dragon known as Vydrarch which was one of the events that lead to the founding of Magnimar.
883935266:
Father's Forge

The Father's Forge is a temple dedicated to Torag in Westgate, whose modest size might cause confusion given that it is the predominant faith of the city's sizable dwarven minority. High Priest Roglund Tergurast explains that every forge and hearth in the city is a shrine to Torag, and the walls display the metal busts of all the priests who have tended the temple for millennia, revealing the quality of its excellent metalwork.
897370226:
Wait's Span

Wait's Span is the name of a tiny hamlet next to a wooden bridge of the same name that sits near the southeastern shore of Lake Kavapesta in the Ustalavic province of Ulcazar. It is located only six miles east of the Monastery of the Veil, and was the site of a massive fire that destroyed most of the village. Although Bishop Senir of the Monastery claims this was a natural event, most locals believe otherwise.

The Wait's Span has since also been referred to as the Senir Bridge and it is noted as being quite a large bridge. Here ancient stones mark the border between the mountain county of Ulcazar and the vales of Amaans. Gargoyles, their faces worn by time, decorate the sides of the high arches of the bridge. It has not been well maintained and it has large crumbling gaps along its length at various places.
919057026:
Shrine of Saint Senex

929785636:
Coastal Cairns

The Coastal Cairns are located near the banks of Lake Encarthan in the nation of Razmiran and are believed to have been built by a race that predates human settlement in the region.
944607476:
Archerus Manor

Archerus Manor is the residence of one of the Inner Sea's most famous artists, the painter Endrik Archerus, who lives in a massive stone tower visible throughout the district.
966110076:
Hollow Mountain

Hollow Mountain is among the highest peaks of Rivenrake Island in the Varisian Gulf. It boasts one of the most intriguing Thassilonian ruins in Varisia. Near the summit of the mountain, an enormous carving of a woman's face looks sternly towards the south, half of her visage rent away by an ancient apocalypse; she looks over the ruins of the bridge that, in ancient times, spanned from Rivenrake to Argavist Island. Exposed underneath, the onlooker can see that countless corridors and rooms must honeycomb the entire mountain.

The aged ruins of what some scholars believe to be the city of Xin-Bakrakhan, the Thassilonian City of Wrath and once the capital of the domain of Bakrakhan, are located at its base. These ruins are said to be one of the few remnants of this mountainous realm which disappeared into the Varisian Gulf following the destruction of the empire in -5293 AR. The split face near the summit is conjectured to be that of Runelord Alaznist herself.

Hollow Mountain was built with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants.
973384156:
Kavapesta

978492236:
Avilan

Avilan is one of the numerous massive trading cities that dot the Qadiran region of the Plains of Paresh, but is often overshadowed by the capital of Katheer. It is located along the northern banks of the Pashman River halfway between Demirah and the capital, Katheer.
1006180616:
Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie

Located in the Docks section of Katapesh Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie is a unique wharf-side attraction that is part museum, part aquarium. For only a single silver piece, anyone can enter and see live fish from all of Golarion's seas, including live piranha from the Mwangi Expanse, a variety of squids and octopuses, and an overweight bunyip. The stuffed attractions are even more impressive, including several intelligent underwater dwellers like ulat-kinis, sedacthys, athamarus, and merfolk. The stuffed collection also includes larger creatures such as a water naga, a young kraken, a mighty alghollthu master, and even an enormous mottled worm.

The Fishy Menagerie is run by Annexa Palanthea, an expert on aquatic life who is said to pay very well for any new and interesting samples—though due to her expansive knowledge, it takes a rare specimen to qualify as either. Annexa sometimes even funds expeditions to search the Inner Sea for new specimens; this interest puts her in competition with the operators of Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep Sea Exploration and Retrieval.
1011695086:
Sunbleached Tower

The Sunbleached Tower is a bizarre location north of the Qadiran city of Dimayen. It is a massive edifice over 300 feet tall made of interlocking human bones that seems to attract daemons.
1047554276:
Consulate of the Platinum Band

The Consulate of the Platinum Band is a temple dedicated to Apsu where his non-draconic followers gather and help others. Although the faith isn't widely understood by the residents of The Puddles, they won't refuse the magical assistance offered. The building also houses archives on the region's dragon lore. The Consulate has few members, distinguished by their pendants bearing the symbol of their god. The rest avoid leaving the temple and do not interact with the Muckruckers or other ruthless groups in the district.
1053770106:
Tamran

5 locations:
  • Encarthan Coast
  • Fort Drejas
  • Fort Machema
  • Purt
  • Spiral of Bones
1060840506:
Sunless Pond

After Earthfall, the Nidalese people faced crop challenges due to the lack of sunlight. Zon-Kuthon provided a solution: the Sunless Pond in the Uskwood. This supernatural spring feeds a pond whose dark water has plant growth-enhancing properties. Shades of the Uskwood druids distribute its water annually to peasants, collecting taxes for the water while assessing the arcane potential of local children. Despite the weak sun's return, farmers still rely on the pond's water to bolster crops for survival, attending gatherings for the precious resource despite the unpleasantness involved.
1062242366:
Jawafeeq

Jawafeeq is a city on the Alavah Peninsula of Qadira.
1062565156:
Fort Ozem

Fort Ozem is a Chernasardo Rangers fortress in Southern Fangwood. Fort Ozem was once used as a staging post in the war against the Whispering Tyrant. It is said that the fort was briefly home to Iomedae herself. The Rangers use it as a hideout. The worn outer walls are overgrown with vegetation. Its appearance has been deliberately neglected to indicate that there is nothing inside worth investigating. If enemies ever do draw near, the fort can be evacuated at a moment's notice.
1062668796:
Gildside

Gildside is a vertical mining town in the southeastern Druman region of Kahlehlin consisting of six levels built into the side of the Rancor Canyon.
1063659956:
Stump

Stump, originally called Egert, is a fishing village and former logging town in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos. It is located northeast of Potter's Lake on the route between Brume and the rest of the Swardlands.
1072975196:
Blackblade's

Blackblade's is a successful fighting academy where former pathfinder and mercenary Benkhal Blackblade instructs his students in combat for five pieces of silver twice a week. Novices spar in the academy's courtyards, serving as an advertisement to attract more students, while the most talented are personally trained by Benkhal inside the building. Most students do not live on the premises, with the exception of the instructors, who at the end of the week lock the doors and meet with former students for parties that last until dawn.
1075340106:
Tower of Winds

The Tower of Winds is a strange structure located on a peninsula on the Plains of Paresh in northwestern Qadira near the Inner Sea's Saray Coast.
1084019956:
Losthome

Losthome is an Ulfen logging town in the far east of the Grungir Forest in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Located on the banks of the Thundering River, though not actually within the Linnorm Kingdom of the Thanelands, Losthome is protected by Kalsgard. Its people, many of them descendants of refugees from the Winter War, constantly watch the border with Irrisen for signs of incursion, and provide sanctuary to anyone fleeing the realm of the Witch Queen.
1084448666:
Resa

Once a fertile village in central Thuvia, Resa is now little more than a flat stretch of desert with a few scattered signs of civilization.

During the decline of Osirion's first age, an Usij necromancer named Aryana Tahari took sanctuary beneath the village and used its inhabitants as test subjects in experiments to create a new plague. These experiments eventually lead to the downfall of the village and its citizens.
1107789716:
Ungkore

The Iblydan city of Ungkore is the capital city of the city-state of Pol-Ungkore. The city, along with the surrounding territory of Pol-Ungkore, has experienced a number of cycles of economic booms and busts. For Ungkore itself, each boom led to widespread building projects, leading to a state today where the city is built upon multiple previous layers, rising up to 300 feet above the area around it. These undercities are a perfect environment for crime to thrive beyond the levels Ungkore can manage.
1121922606:
Elith Lorin

Elith Lorin is one of the most pleasant settlements in Nidal, and is one of the most important transportation hubs in the nation.
1125799966:
Mercy

Mercy is a small town about 20 miles south of Alkenstar and a mile south of the Ustradi River. It is made up of a wooden palisade that surrounds the roughly one hundred buildings.
1137064176:
Talamir

Talamir, now known as Dragonscar, was a wealthy city whose prideful citizens once painted many of its buildings green and blue to match the flag of Taldor. It is now an ash-encrusted ruin charred by both volcanic and draconic flame.
1165284186:
Glimmerhold

Glimmerhold is a small, independent dwarven city. As of 4715 AR, it had been located within the borders of the human country of Nirmathas, near its border at the time with Nidal and prior to the establishment of Oprak.
1176540206:
Merotsi Mines

The Merotsi Mines are a series of abandoned mines in the Footprints of Rovagug that contained a Rovagug spawn born in a pool of acid. Located about 30 miles from Tar Kuata, the miners abandoned the area after striking a thermal zone that filled the mine with toxic gases, and it was abandoned over a century ago. In 4715 AR, a Rovagug cult led by Tasutek took over the mines to use as a temple, until the Pathfinder Society sent agents to rescue their victims and destroy them.
1188020996:
Imvrildara

The ancient Calistrian temple of Imvrildara, located in the southern Fierani Forest of Kyonin, is the base for the goddess's most militant sect of worshipers: the Imvrildarai, defenders of the forest against Treerazer and its demonic armies.
1188186746:
Mishkar

Unusually for a human community in Iobaria, Mishkar is not built from the scavenged remains of cyclopean ruins. Originally established in 809 AR as a guard post against threats from the Grenalf Plateau, it was abandoned by Iobaria around 4607 AR, only to be re-established by Varisian and Taldan settlers around 4659 AR.

The town has since become a largely self-sufficient city-state with a large agricultural and trade base. The people of Mishkar greatly value their independence and have remained unaffiliated with Iobaria's various factions, though they have forged an occasional alliance with centaur tribes against goblin hordes from the highlands.

In 4719 AR, the town was damaged by a massive forest fire in the nearby Finadar Forest, but had largely recovered by 4720 AR.
1188846946:
Toduc Wa

Toduc Wa (pronounced tu-duk wah) was once the largest city in the Tian Xia nation of Linvarre, which was formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar. The Monastery of the Blended Forms, a renowned martial arts retreat inspired by Taldor's Tian-founded Monastery of the Seven Forms, is located in Toduc Wa.
1211308316:
Palace of Thirteen Spires

The Palace of Thirteen Spires is a small but opulent palace in the Wise Quarter, divided into four wings, each in a Chelaxian, Osirian, Taldan, and Minkan style. For most of the year, the rooms are rented to merchants and nobles visiting the city. The White Grotto holds the contract to the estate, working with the Wise Council to manage the building exclusively, a fact that greatly displeases the Arcanamirium. When a head of state visits, everyone else is evicted so the best rooms can be reserved for royalty, and the money raised goes toward public works and scholarships.
1211754706:
Vaneo Tilernos

Vaneo Tilernos is one of the smaller mansions in the area, a magnificent structure of wood, glass, and a gold-painted roof that gleams all day. It is the residence of Lord-Mayor Kajen Tilernos, and due to the explosion of 4710 AR that destroyed the traditional mayor's home, this residence has served as his official residence ever since.
1215897726:
Sunhill Monastery

Sunhill Monastery is located on a plateau just west of the Zho Mountains of Qadira. The complex contains chapels to both the empyreal lord Ragathiel and the goddess Sarenrae. It is the headquarters of the Eleli who are overseen by a peri named Nuryah.
1223604436:
Vizio's Tavern

Vizio's Tavern is an establishment in Rego Scripa whose former owners left for Corentyn after the death of a family member, leaving the tavern empty. It was bought by a couple who used it for their confidential meetings to plan acts of rebellion.
1234263756:
Stilt House

The Stilt House is a city-owned building in the Puddles district of Absalom that serves as the headquarters of the district guard, the Muckruckers. It also serves as the headquarters of the Salvage Guild. Haigen Topkick, the Nomarch of the Puddles District Council and also captain of the Muckruckers and guildmaster of the Salvage Guild, can be usually found in the Stilt House.
1245710786:
Hook Mountain

Hook Mountain is the southern-most peak of central Varisia's Iron Peaks, located just south of the Storval Deep and north of the expansive Sanos Forest. It is synonymous with the Kreegs, the most bloodthirsty clan of ogres on Golarion, who until recently claimed the region around the mountain as their personal domain.
1256152756:
Dragon's Gullet

The Dragon's Gullet is an expansive tunnel which lies deep under the Sanctum of the Sorrowmaker on Dahak's Fang island in the Shackles. Aashaq sleeps and feeds in the Sanctum but she spends the majority of her time gloating over her precious hoard in the Dragon's Gullet. The entrance to the tunnel is a cave mouth at the bottom of Lake Rogshagrox which leads to a vertical cavern over 4 miles in length. The heat at the bottom of the Dragon's Gullet is unbearable.

Morilla Palace and the Tally Wall

1274501276:
Kragnaroth

The fire giant kingdom of Kragnaroth is located in the Mindspin Mountains, east of Varisia. Its current ruler is King Orynox Marchelin, a fierce and ruthless warlord who has plans to expand Kragnaroth across the Mindspin Mountains both south and east. Orynox's latest queen is Hregana and he seems to be especially preoccupied by her. He has been married to her longer than his previous five wives combined.

Outsiders would be very confused by the giants referring to this place as a "kingdom", as it is not much bigger than a large city that spans about 15 square miles. It consists of three towers arranged around a valley that house about 250 fire giants, with hundreds of smaller humanoid slaves who serve as farmers and in forges.
1277986886:
Grandfather's Arms

Grandfather's Arms refers to the settled area between two volcanic ridges north and northwest of the Weeping Grandfather volcano on the Isle of Kortos. The Earthsong tribe of minotaurs have lived in Grandfather's Arms for generations, farming the rich soil and crafting items for trade out of bone and stone.
1286200596:
Cavern of Many Mouths

The Cavern of Many Mouths is located on the Lady's Cape of southwestern Varisia, near the site of the Lady's Light. It is named after the pyrohydra Gegganallag—Draconic for "many mouths"—who as of 4712 AR laired there and was venerated by local xulgaths. The cavern is one of several that connect to tunnels which run beneath the Lady's Lagoon.
1288526686:
Mermaid's Bucket

The Mermaid's Bucket is a famous tavern located next to the main docks of Crescent Harbor in the pirate city of Port Peril. A rowdy bar, the Mermaid's Bucket is decorated with numerous depictions of merfolk, fish masks, and other nautical paraphernalia of all sorts, including an enormous mosaic of a mermaid behind the bar. The Mermaid's Bucket's proprietor Nefti Unwesha also supplies rum to every bar, tavern, and brothel in town, buying out her competition with strangely minted silver coins.

Century Root

The Century Root is a massive, half-mile-wide tree stump located in the southern Fierani Forest of Kyonin, just south and east of a bend in the Kyonin River. As of 4709 AR it was the location of a arboreal regent moot, a meeting of these woodland protectors from across the forest.

1320500196:
Thraxnorni

Thraxnorni, also known as the "Bone Caverns" are a series of caverns that sprawl beneath Iobaria's Fangard Forest. A cairn located a short distance away from a crossroad of the Koloran Roads within the forest marks the entrance to the caverns, though other sinkholes that lead to their dark depths are thought to exist. The caverns get their name from stories that say they are lined from floor to ceiling with bones, the victims of the undead and other menaces that dwell within.
1322743456:
Metringer Sanitarium

Metringer Sanitarium has been in operation since 4000 AR, serving as a place of healing and medicine for those suffering from mental illness, as well as a site for ethically questionable experiments on these patients. Its director, Flevvid Grummlin, has shown the facility to the Sally Guard on several occasions, but they have found nothing out of the ordinary. In secret dungeons, patients deemed unfit for service are taken to end their stay. Salindra Concilio escaped the building and has been warning others and seeking help to reveal the situation inside, though many consider her delusional. Only one person is offering a reward for this information.

1373868146:
Hagby

The small trading town of Hagby is located on Glacier Lake in the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold.

Hagby is the last stop for all trade headed to Whitethrone from the west. It is ruled with paranoid scrutiny by the cruel Baroness Ladina. The peasants do not trust anyone knowing that even a hint of the slightest misdemeanor results in harsh punishments. A lot of peasants work their daily duties with missing fingers, eye patches and worse. Yefrem Chelnitsky is the despicable agent of the baroness. He follows her everywhere, with parchment and quill in hand to record every demand and directive his mistress utters.

Despite the overbearing atmosphere, the Heralds of Summer's Return have a lot of members in Hagby including warehouse worker Nakia Serebrynakova and blacksmith Yuri Patasil.
1378422966:
Edme

The city of Edme, in the easternmost reaches of Galt north of the Boarwood, was once a great center of learning.
1378628356:
Crimson Coin

Crimson Coin is a bustling two-story tavern and gambling house located in the Foreign Quarter in the city of Absalom. It was one of the bars at which the Pathfinder Society was founded on 23 Desnus, 4307 AR.
1387336016:
Wreck of the Jenivere

1 locations:

1390567606:
Three Pines Ford

Three Pines Ford is a sizable town nestled on the Tourondel River of Lastwall, east of the forest of Fangwood, and directly on its border with Nirmathas. It serves as a meeting-place for Lastwall officials and fresh recruits from Nirmathas. Heroes of Nirmathas use Three Pines Ford as a hiding place from agents of Molthune, due to the town's close proximity to the Nirmathi border.
1402075236:
Bleachbone Cave

Bleachbone Cave is a concealed cavern located in the Vergan Forest in Razmiran. This cave remains shrouded in secrecy, its sole entrance artfully hidden by an enormous, bone-shaped white rock. Few outside of its clandestine inhabitants are even aware of its existence, rendering it an ideal refuge for those seeking to elude the ever-watchful eye of the Razmiri clergy.

In recent times, a small group from Whispertruth, a faction dedicated to opposing the rule of the Living God Razmir, has sought sanctuary within Bleachbone Cave. Korellen Duinsol, an aiuvarin ranger, leads this band of rebels. Concerned about Razmir's apparent knowledge of their activities and the countermeasures he has been taking, the group has chosen to keep their newfound sanctuary a closely guarded secret.

Unbeknownst to the rebels and their captive, a malevolent presence known only as the Ancient One resides in the deepest recesses of the cave, patiently waiting. It comes from the Dominion of the Black and patiently waits for the right living mortal to possess in order to escape its trap in this cave.
1411240706:
Soggy Piper

The Soggy Piper is a tavern perched on a cliff in The Puddles, a hangout for criminals and cultists. Violent crime is commonplace there, as bodies can be disposed of by simply pushing them off the cliff into the sea. The patrons avoid eye contact, creating a moody atmosphere and while the food is fairly average, it's a good place to gather information about the district's activities.

1434687186:
Drillstones

The Drillstones is one of the many Thassilonian ruins in the wilds of the Linnorm Kingdom of Southmoor. More than three dozen spire-like, 60-foot-tall stone monoliths rise from the northern Kodar Mountains foothills 50 miles southeast of Kopparberget. Each of these monoliths is decorated with strange spiralling gouges, almost as if they were immense drills sent up from underground. Each stone vibrates slightly to the touch, but some nights of the year these vibrations increase into a disturbing buzz. The stones' purpose is unknown, but a mysterious doorway was recently discovered at the base of one of the monoliths that leads to a strange labyrinth, and deactivated clockworks were found inside. Some scholars believe that the Drillstones were the upper edge of a war machine buried underground.
1440471006:
Wormwood Hall

Wormwood Hall is located deep within the Lurkwood of north-western Varisia, near the forest's border with the Red Mountains. A large manor house overrun by chocking vines, no being has set foot within the hall in living memory. Strange, twisting runes cover its doors and walls, and it is generally assumed that Wormwood is tied to the Lurkwood's strange seasons.
1448131986:
Greenglade

The Greenglade is a way station on the Marideth River near the city of Tamran in southern Nirmathas. Once a month, it also hosts a bustling market in smuggled weapons and intelligence, particularly for valuable information about the Molthuni Army.
1452373226:
Zilpatzi

Zilpatzi is a city in Xopatl that has suffered greatly since the nearby Manzinago Forest became too dangerous to log. Before the Deepweave came to the Manzinago Forest, much of its wealth came from boat caravans that would float down the rivers of Xopatl accompanying the floating cut wood. Since they can no longer safely make lumber, the focus of these floating caravans has now pivoted to focus entirely on trade and shipping goods up and down the length of the rivers. These voyages can sometimes venture further afield across all of Arcadia and can last months at a time. Within the city, local legend claims that some kind of smoke dragon lives beneath the local church of Cihua Coatl.
1454024076:
Balmore's Canal

Balmore's Canal is an artificial waterway that bisects the Tamuth District in the southwest corner of the Andoren capital of Almas. Originally built to transport merchant barges, the canal has become a repulsive dumping ground for industrial waste from Smithtown, and more organic waste from Mugget. The canal's appearance and stench have become an embarrassment for the city, but no one has come up with a satisfying solution for the problem.
1458563546:
Ghash

Ghash is the name given to both the mountain and the small wooded valley that lies within it. It is located in the Ulcazar peaks of the Hungry Mountains, in the nation of Ustalav.

Quantium

1474911526:
Blackwood Moot

Blackwood Moot is an isolated stand of Blackwood trees deep in the Verduran Forest of Taldor that serves as a meeting place for wild residents dissatisfied with the Treaty of the Wildwood. Those who meet at Blackwood Moot view the members of the Wildwood Lodge as traitors who have enabled Taldor's destruction of the Verduran Forest.

The Blackwood Moot is led by the arboreal regent Oakadence, a powerful sorcerer who has been organising secretive raids on Wispil and the Isle of Arenway using the wild beasts of the forest as disposable foot soldiers.
1485020976:
Mzali

Mzali is the largest Garundi city south of Katapesh in the Inner Sea region and is ruled by the undead god-emperor named Walkena. Having risen from death to destroy Avistani looters who sought to plunder Mzali's riches, Walkena became the absolute ruler of the city. The child-god rules Mzali with tyranny and cruelty, killing any who question his rule or even trade with non-Mwangi peoples. The city is an immense metropolis, with numerous ancient crypts and tombs beneath the city.
1495579936:
Scrag Deep

Scrag Deep is an extensive underwater system of caverns at the deepest point of the Glacier Lake in Irrisen.

In the Winter War a tribe of scrags under the command of the great warlord Tirchagac, fought the longships of the Djurstor Confederacy on Glacier Lake. As a reward, Baba Yaga gave the scrags dominion over the Scrag Deep. Scrag Deep is still home of the Scrag Deep tribe descendants of the allies of Baba Yaga.
1504340436:
Tomb of the Living

The Tomb of the Living is located in Westgate and is built in the Osirian style. Thanks to the loophole that those who continue to move when entombed can be imprisoned without trial after being buried, the city maintains that this is a tomb, not a prison and its spaciousness allows them to incarcerate enemies of the city indefinitely, whenever they wish, without technically breaking the laws established by Aroden. The prison's existence is a secret from the masses and other nations, who would not react well to its presence. The city performs rituals in the upper crypts, which contain the corpses of nobles who lacked the means for burial elsewhere in the city and below lies a labyrinth of cells accessible only to members of the Sally Guard, those who reveal its secret are punished by being imprisoned in the tomb. The darkness, scarce food and water, and limited space drive those imprisoned here insane, causing their muscles to atrophy and constantly weakened.

Some of the prisoners here whose names have been forgotten include:

  • The Almost Goddess, around 4700 AR, a charismatic woman who arrived in Absalom to take the Test of the Starstone. She amassed a large following and was talented in everything she did; no one doubted she would become a goddess. On the day she was to take the test, she disappeared and was forgotten.
  • The Forever Wizard, a researcher obsessed with his own mortality, tried to overcome it, and surprisingly, contrary to what usually happens with these immortality schemes, he achieved eternal life. After experimenting on his own body, he achieved perfect regeneration, making it impossible for him to die from violence or old age. He was going to leave to go see his elderly parents in Taldor to share the secret of immortality with them and then with the rest of the world. He disappeared the day before he was to leave by ship.
  • The Quiet King, a nobleman of the city, witnessed all the atrocities Absalom committed for the "good of the nation", and his conscience wouldn't allow him to let this continue. He spoke to journalists, telling them he would bring them proof of these atrocities and that they should wait for him. Now he is in a cell under constant magical silence, and his tongue has been cut out.
1515813446:
Bacul Gruii

The village of Bacul Gruii was taken over by Petchki, a foreign merchant, who enslaved the locals to harvest wood and livestock for his band of mercenaries. Petchki's men slew Otok, the village chief, and mounted his head in the town square to keep the villagers afraid. Petchki pays the villagers next to nothing and only does so to avoid violating the fifth River Freedom prohibiting slavery. To legitimize his rule over Bacul Gruii, the mercenary captain plans to marry one of Otok's daughters and declare himself the new village chief.
1522131856:
Crestwatch

Crestwatch is a structure with a 100-foot minaret in the middle of The Docks that serves as the administrative center of the district. The east wing is used by the Harbor Guard, which patrols and fights crime, and the west wing is used by the Docks Council, which creates the laws and taxes that apply to ships docking in the harbor. There are tensions about having criminal elements in the same building as those who administer the port.
1522469576:
Nokkaichi

Nokkaichi (pronounced nohk-KAI-chee) is a fishing city located in the nation of Chu Ye. It is rich with aquatic life due to the presence of a magical cold-water reef full of kami, similar in nature to the Forest of Spirits on land. The reef's kami have become hostile to fishers due to overfishing and the presence of oni.

Ecanus

Ecanus is a fortress-city in southern Nex that was established during the centuries-long war with neighbouring Geb. It is a sprawling city that serves as the hub of Nex's arcane-powered military. Here the battle mages that make up the backbone of the army are trained in a mixture of potent evocation and battle tactics. The city is also famous for its fleshforges: building-sized artefacts created by the archmage Nex himself that provide the terrifying war beasts which support the battle mages. The fleshforges are responsible for many of the nightmarish creatures which roam the wastelands of Nex, the Barrier Wall mountains, the Mana Wastes, and even the Mwangi Expanse.

The city is the terminus of a trade route that winds its way south through the Mana Wastes, ending at the Gebbite town of Graydirge.

Willowside

Willowside is an old, reclusive town on the northwestern coast of the Isle of Kortos, which has suffered greatly from a strange phenomenon known as the Welt in recent years.
1564290196:
Gutless Griffon

The Gutless Griffon is a tavern in the Ivy District that has become very popular thanks to its owner, Fronsac Shimm. He chooses the food and the music, but above all, it's his lively attitude that makes him so appealing. He introduces himself to new patrons by telling anecdotes or serving drinks. His popularity even earned him a seat on the Grand Council, giving him a rags-to-riches story that only makes him more likable. The establishment boasts a taproom and balconies furnished with the finest furniture, and bards who are excluded from the Street Performers and Actors' Guild are welcome. Shimm has a mutual animosity with Alain Always, even though they were once best friends. They respect each other and generally don't interfere in each other's affairs, but since the Brotherhood of Abadar often hangs out at the tavern, the rift between them only widens. One of the first things you notice upon entering is its large fireplace and hearth, where the craziest stories from all over the Inner Sea are told.
1576371726:
Cutter's Holdout

Cutter's Holdout is a small village near the borders of Druma and Kyonin, and has been disputed territory between the two nations since the return of elves to Golarion from Castrovel in 2632 AR.
1577135216:
Deromas

Little is known about Deromas, a settlement of western Osirion during its First Age located in what is now Rahadoum. The city was known for its lawful nature and situated on the southern banks of the Uta River.
1608629216:
Niswan

The port city of Niswan is the capital of the Impossible Kingdom of Jalmeray. Niswan is a many-tiered city of pagodas renowned for its beauty. Every building supports colourful silk banners that flutter in the cold ocean winds, and red stone streets crisscross the hillsides. Cosmopolitan Niswan is a meeting place for travellers and scholars from all over Golarion, and offers sights seen nowhere else in the region. Here, gold-draped eunuchs walk the streets, and Vudran princes offering strange tribute to statues of gods utterly unknown in this region. Niswan is renowned as a centre of religious learning: its High-Holy district is home to scholars and gurus renowned as some of the wisest people on the face of Golarion, and the city is dotted with numerous shrines, statues, and temples dedicated to the hundreds of deities of the Vudran pantheon.

1621996576:
Ledenica

Ledenica is a small, yet busy town on the Marbleflow River in the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold. The town is fortified against the wintry terrors originating in the Frozen Fog across the river.

Baroness Helka is the efficient ruler of the town and her stone keep is located in the center of Ledenica. The Baroness, convinced by her Jadwiga guard captain Vlad Omelinsky, hires mercenaries to deal with the threats from the Frozen Fog, rather than risk her soldiers. Vlad Omelinsky handles the contracts with the mercenaries and is notorious for cheating and filling his own coffers. The Baroness knows nothing about this and, if she were ever made aware, she would execute her captain.

Ledenica is famous for three of its inns: Natalya's Riverside Inn, the Spirit's Caress and the White Raven. The travellers to and from Whitethrone enjoy visiting these inns. The White Raven is owned and run by sisters Kira Brodovsky and Kaleena Brodovsky and is a secret meeting place for the underground anti-government movement called the Heralds of Summer's Return.
1634374826:
Rozenport

Rozenport is a bustling town located in the south-west of the county of Versex in Ustalav perched high on the steep cliffs above the Danver River. The town is most famous as the seat of the Sincomakti School of Sciences founded in 4570 AR. Many of the buildings of the town mirror the archaic architectural style of the world-renowned school, for instance, pointed steeples and manor houses topped with flat roofs. The town hall boasts a dome shaped like a bubble. All this is set within a veritable maze of roads and thoroughfares.
1637041086:
Staviancara

Staviancara is the oldest inhabited building in Westcrown, having been built by Taldan slaves, which gives it a military architecture with secret tunnels beneath. It serves as the residence of the Regidottari commander of the city.
1643386466:
Guiding Hand

The Guiding Hand is an establishment that offers food, shelter, and job opportunities in the poverty-stricken neighborhood of Mudhaven. Tern on the Wind is an elf from the Crown of the World who came to represent her insular people in the city, but upon witnessing the plight of the people in the streets, she began her religious mission to help the community. She follows Findeladlara, and the two-story building with stained-glass windows is kept clean thanks to magical wards amidst the filthy streets and buildings.

Wirholt's Rictus

Wirholt's Rictus is a farm near the Mindspin Mountains primarily dedicated to cultivating crops that can be processed into medicine and drugs. The farm sells its products to the Nidalese, though some foreign clients seeking to experience drug-induced pain also place orders. The owner is Temarra, a devoted follower of Zon-Kuthon, who punishes her employees to keep them docile.

Beneath the farm are laboratories used by the Korholm Agenda to further their research.
1667924176:
Avatectura Museum

The Avatectura Museum in the Wise Quarter offers city archives dating from all eras, although its popular style is due to its curator, Dhauken Tor, who manages it.
1675339906:
Oaktop Silver Mine

The Oaktop Silver Mine, or Sootscale caverns, as named after their current inhabitants, is an abandoned silver mine in the side of a hill just north of the Shrike River. A kobold tribe named Sootscales has lived in the mine for decades now, surviving by catching fish in the nearby river.
1675919806:
Sostarlannex

Sostarlannex is an annex between the Canal Wisxar and the Canal Thrachi, featuring a grime wall which still retains the bas-reliefs depicting the exploits of the Great Drought, sculpted by Sostarl.
1676536016:
Drowning Rock

Drowning Rock is a small town on the southwestern side of the island of Tempest Cay in the Shackles. Its position largely shields it from the stormy winds of the north.

The town is built around a towering monolith that gradually sinks into the muddy soil, and the townsfolk hold their landmark in high esteem. Every summer the town throws a two-day festival, and the main event involves the strongest men coming together and hoisting the rock from the muck via rope and pulley. The town economy is based on its farms and clam fields.
1677719696:
Shileh

Shileh is one of the numerous large, prosperous cities that dot the fertile Qadiran region of Pashman. It is located north of the banks of the Pashman River along Pashman's south-western border with the neighbouring region of Tapur.
1688857516:
Sueda Lodge

Sueda Lodge is a Pathfinder lodge in southeastern Katapesh that aids Pathfinders and other explorers searching for ancient Osirian artifacts, and also serves as a repository for information.
1714291736:
Ashpeak

The Ashpeak is a dormant volcano in the Mindspin Mountains, where clans of fire giants have resided for generations. The volcano rises approximately 7,000 feet and boasts large forests at its base. Numerous tunnels have been renovated by the giants, serving as fortresses. They have created openings that allow rainwater to pass through, and various signs have been placed to guide them through the labyrinthine tunnels.
1716016586:
Golushkin

Golushkin is a dwarven settlement located in the Golushkin Mountains. The Golka clan leads the settlement. These dwarves had a trade relationship with House Garess of Brevoy, but all contact has been lost from roughly the same time as the Vanishing of Brevoy's House Rogarvia.
1729275346:
Zincher's Camp

Zincher's Camp is the area designated by Clegg Zincher after he was taken over by the drow, as it was the closest location to the sea caves where they hid. Two hunters' houses are located in the area, though they are in danger of collapse, so no one uses them for shelter.
1729528136:
Everpine

Everpine is an unusual fey settlement in the western Barrowood of Cheliax.

1736219996:
Tallavont School

The Tallavont School was founded centuries ago by idealists seeking to foster a sense of community in Eastgate by creating an institution that would allow teachers persecuted in their home countries for their political beliefs to teach here instead of being entangled in the city's treacherous and infighting politics. Called "the Fortress" because it incorporates an old war keep from the city's early history, this structure defines the district's skyline and is visible for miles around. Teachers often use the height to give lessons from the balconies, offering views of the city and providing their students with a unique teaching perspective. A quarter of the students live in Eastgate, while the rest come from noble families in the city or abroad who send their children to study here.
1752514506:
Forgotten Track

The Forgotten Track is a prison mine located at the bottom of a large canyon in a region of Razmiran known as the Tracks. Those who have lost faith in or displease the Church of Razmir are sent here; it is called the Forgotten Track because that is the eventual fate of most who are sent there.
1765694296:
Sokar's Boil

Sokar's Boil is one of Osirion's two volcanoes—the other is the nearby Asuulek's Mouth. Both are located in the country's southwestern region known as the Footprints of Rovagug. Sokar's Boil (or just the Boil as it is sometimes called) is currently dormant. The last eruption took place some three thousand years ago and was extremely violent.
1792665996:
Pridon's Hearth

Pridon's Hearth marked the most southerly point of expansion of the historical Chelaxian colony of Sargava, now known as the independent nation of Vidrian.
1809772906:
Daggermark

The largest city in all of the River Kingdoms, Daggermark has maintained enough political and economic stability to secure an abundant supply of food, a rarity in the chaotic Broken Lands region of northeastern Avistan. Daggermark is so stable that it even has a thriving industry and is wealthy enough to mint its own currency.

The main reason for this stability are the two guilds for which the city is so famous: the Daggermark Assassins' Guild and the Daggermark Poisoners' Guild. These two organizations often work hand in hand, as the assassins favor the use of poisons, believing that only amateurs leave behind an obvious cause of death. Few bandit lords would be stupid enough to attack Daggermark for fear of being found poisoned, strangled, or having succumbed to some other seemingly "accidental" death.
1810577356:
Herne's Oak

Herne's Oak is a barrow deep within the Estrovian Forest of Mendev and the final resting place of the ill-fated hunter Herne Vilhaur. It is guarded by his descendants that were transformed by the curse of the winterthorn: the herne.
1825207356:
Fort Trevalay

Fort Trevalay is the only fortress that the Chernasardo Rangers built entirely themselves. It is located in the Southern Fangwood and is constructed atop a massive stone column in the center of a spacious and deep gorge formed by the Sixjaws River. Fort Trevalay was built in 4687 AR of the same light gray stone that it stands upon and is accessible only via rope bridges. It was designed from the start to fully meet the rebels' needs. It features a training hall, war room, and forge as well as many other necessary accommodations. It is the most defensible of the Chernasardo Rangers' forts. The buildings within the fort are 10 feet high, except for the main tower which rises to a height of 45 feet. The stone curtain wall around the fort is 12 feet high and rises from the edge of the stone pillar upon which the fort is built.

Tsering Springs

The Tsering Springs (pronounced SEAR-ing Springs) are the ancestral home of the samsarans of Zi Ha in Tian Xia. The settlement allows entry to outsiders only on invitation or when accompanied by a samsaran guide.
