Hillcross

Not so much a true city as a stable, permanent location for a vast tent city, Hillcross serves as a meeting ground for the various nomadic tribes of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords.
1856422836:
Xin-Haruka

Xin-Haruka was the capital of the Thassilonian kingdom of Haruka, dedicated to sloth. Its Runelords disregarded the mundane necessities of daily life and kept a multitude of servants and slaves who performed physical labor for them. The last Runelord of Sloth Krune, peacefully ascended the throne, succeeding Ivarinna and became the high priest of the goddess Lissala. A large number of summoned outsiders lived in the city, and a network of teleporters connected it to other regions or distant planes. Invisible servants kept the streets spotless and many kholos also lived in the city, serving in its armies, though their lazy nature did not make them good soldiers. The faiths of Lissala and the Peacock Spirit clashed in the streets, sometimes with oratory and other times with violence, and one of the largest temples dedicated to the goddess in Thassilon was located in the city. Several miles away, Desgard's Thousand Columns maintained a reserve of thousands of extraplanar soldiers that Krune could deploy in case of rebellion or external attack.
1873952916:
Cypress Point

The small town of Cypress Point, located about 40 miles northeast of Kintargo, on the point of land furthest north on the coast, is the largest settlement in Ravounel east of the Yolubilis River.

Pergamon Pass

Pergamon Pass is a route through the south of the World's Edge Mountains in Taldor. The closest settlement to the pass is the town of Dalaston.

Dubrov

Dubrov is a small village on the western edge of the Estrovian Forest in Mendev. During the Mendevian Crusades, its main industry was supplying the holy warriors with arrows, bows, and bowstrings. Morcei

Morcei was a quiet hamlet in the east of the Ustalavic county of Lozeri, situated under the south-eastern branches of the vast Shudderwood; the Mothburn has its source nearby. Fort Dagh

Fort Dagh is located at the summit of a mountain peak south of Tar-Kazmukh and overlooks the Great Pass of the northwestern Five Kings Mountains. A nearby peak bears the Carved Kings sculpture of King Kazmukh of Doggadth, which also overlooks the Great Pass.

Omesta

Omesta is a town in the northwestern Fierani Forest of Kyonin, inhabited primarily by treehouse-dwelling gnomes, who have been living there since before the elves' return to Golarion.
1982176826:
Vigil

Vigil was not only a mighty fortress that drew warriors of good like a beacon, but also the capital of the country of Lastwall. It was a city of music, joy, and faith that came to a terrible end in the summer of 4719 AR when Tar-Baphon escaped from his centuries-long confinement and unleashed the destructive weapon known as the Radiant Fire upon the city, destroying it completely. Its ruined remains now mar the Gravelands.
2026743386:
Kavapesta

Kavapesta is the population center for the Ustalavic county of Amaans, located on the shores of Lake Kavapesta.

The largest city in Amaans and a holy city for Pharasmins, Kavapesta is a somber place possessed by a dour variety of religious fanaticism. The city takes its name from Mother Kavapesta, a Pharasmin missionary and teacher whose sermons on perseverance and suffering laid the foundations for the philosophies today known as the Pharasmin Penitence. After the priestess's death, her followers began the construction of Cryptgate Cathedral, which through centuries of renovations and expansions would become the largest church and monastery of Pharasma in Ustalav. The effort of constructing this monument and the tombs beneath brought droves of pilgrims to the banks of Lake Divirmis, which the faithful eventually renamed Lake Kavapesta. With the cathedral's completion, the assembled congregation lingered on, hoping to create a community based on the tenets of their faith. This community grew slowly, finally becoming the city known today.

Yet a city needs more than faith to survive, a fact the people of Kavapesta stubbornly defy. The foothills around the city are poorly suited to farming and the lake is tainted by poisonous minerals from the Hungry Mountains. A few mines in the surrounding hills scrape tin and coal out of the earth, though one has to wonder if the coin won from these efforts is worth the lives lost to cave-ins, choking maladies, and more mysterious disappearances. But worst of all are the plagues, the rampant sicknesses that seem to boil up from Lake Kavapesta once a generation and burn through the city like a field fire. Scourges like the Whithers, Tol's Voice, and, most fearfully, the Bleak Breath have killed thousands in their beds, with lesser scares and re-emergences occurring every few years. But the people claim that their city is blessed—not cursed like so many visitors say—insisting that each new calamity is a test from Pharasma and an opportunity for the citizens to experience ever greater trials, assuring greater rewards after death.
2027597726:
House of Yanti Purnállo

The House of Yanti Purnállo is the home of the last remaining hero-god of Xopatl, Purnállo. While most of the hero-gods are mythic figures of ancient history who have long since ascended to true godhood, disappeared, or died, Purnállo chose a different path. Thousands of years ago, Purnállo undertook a unique process: she began to meditate while ritually wrapping herself in the enhanced vines and leaves of Xopatl's plant life, eventually effectively mummifying herself while still alive. This mummification resulted in Purnállo reducing even her essential life processes to an almost imperceptibly slow speed. Over the centuries the people of Xopatl erected a temple around the remains of Purnállo and now the people flock in pilgrimage to the House of Yanti Purnállo to hear the saint's heart beat once per century.
2032008706:
Fierce Stripe

The Fierce Stripe is a shop where all of Absalom's badger needs are met. Lady Chesne of House Yuriel runs the shop, acquiring unique breeds of badgers for adoption by the residents of Westgate, where the animal has supplanted dogs and cats as a pet. The Tri-Stripe Society shops there and meets weekly to drink tea and discuss the town's badger competitions.
2037420076:
New Jelheg

New Jelheg is a very small settlement in southern Druma.
2044280326:
Narona

The ruined city of Narona is located on Hellmouth Gulf on the western coast of Cheliax on the Arcadian Ocean. The city was destroyed by the Chelaxian Navy because of its resistance against the rule of House Thrune. Unlike the inhabitants of the nearby city of Pezzack, Narona's residents never returned after the settlement's destruction.
2053310916:
Whitemound

Whitemound is a town nestled in the foothills of the Mindspin Mountains, on the border between the Atteran Ranches and the Uthori Steppes in the far north of Nidal. The town boasts a thriving economy thanks to its granary and serves as a popular stop for ranchers to sell their produce or to hold ceremonies, such as weddings. It is also famous for its tanners' leather goods.

Umbral Court agent Insevier of Nisroch is officially the town's leader, though this is more of a punishment imposed upon him due to several failed missions. The citizens of Whitemound generally avoid him, only interacting with him during sacred celebrations of Zon-Kuthon. Insevier is tasked with patrolling the Uthori Pass to prevent citizens from escaping to Korvosa, but in recent years he has not had much to do, as a leucrotta known as the Ascian, has been hunting down anyone attempting to cross, especially those from Whitemound.

2063764966:
Salmon

The Salmon is one of the ten remaining pilings of the Irespan in Magnimar. It stands 220 feet high. There are no known entrances into the piling.
2074637036:
Vault of the Impostor Goblin

No one is entirely sure what is inside the Vault of the Imposter Goblin. A few even doubt its existence, though it certainly does. The vault has one large stone entryway that connects to a larger underground complex.
2076230286:
Yled

Yled (pronounced EH-lehd) is the largest city in the land of Geb and also home to most of its undead armies.
2082137486:
Spiral of Bones

The Spiral of Bones is a communal cemetery on the south-eastern edge of the Fangwood where 333 Nirmathi rebels who died during the first assault on Tamran in 4646 AR are buried. While the graves were being dug, a sinkhole opened up, swallowing the corpses. Because they were preoccupied with the war, the hole was never excavated, nor were the bodies recovered, and rumors of vengeful spirits keep people away from the site. The area inside the sinkhole overlooks a hryngar settlement that was abandoned when the local ore ran out, though recently a group of prospectors, led by a hryngar named Asdynn the Breaker, has been attempting to reclaim the citadel. Because many monsters from the Darklands have claimed parts of the citadel, clearing it will take a considerable amount of time.
2087549696:
Doorway to the Red Star

The Doorway to the Red Star is a ring of mysterious, humming red stones in an empty courtyard surrounded by overgrown cathedrals. It is located north of Lake Ocota in the Mwangi Expanse.
2106409386:
Soth-Silir

Soth-Silir is a 30-foot-tall, oddly shaped monument on the northern edge of the Ombrefell, guarded by the Xoskerik tribe of shadow giants led by their priest Salifeth. Several shelters serve as homes for the shadow giants, who patrol the Atteran Ranches, inspiring both respect and fear. The sacrifice of Nidalese heretics is common at the monument and their provision forms part of the Xoskerik's agreement with the Umbral Court. Not all heretics are suitable for sacrifice: some are eaten instead; those deemed unfit are exiled to Varisia through the Uthori Pass.
2106483436:
Mount Ebaios

Mount Ebaios is the largest volcano in Iblydos located on the north of the island of Nenakari. The volcano's name is used as an aphorism for a cure that comes with horrible side effects. It gets this reputation from the lava that has oozed out of it for the past thousand years that is said to come from the Iblydan underworld or some other evil realm. This lava burns and traps any creature that comes near it as if in amber, only for that creature to re-awaken some time later as an undead creature that breathes out noxious fumes.
2110361106:
Raseri Kanton

Raseri Kanton in the Five Kings Mountains was the sister city of Jernashall. Khadon the Mighty founded the empire of Tar Khadurrm in 3279 AR and the city of Jernashall in 3312 AR. Jernashall became the preeminent city of the new empire, although Highhelm remained its capital. Jernashall became so important that the nations of Cheliax and Isger petitioned it to help them build a surface city close to it. With the help of the dwarves, the city of Raseri Kanton was founded in 3451 AR, and became a vital trade center in the region.

For 500 years, Tar Khadurrm and its cities flourished, until the Rending of Droskar's Crag in 3980 AR. The seismic and volcanic event destroyed Jernashall and its sister city of Raseri Kanton. In Jernashall, a river of lava killed every citizen within minutes. The fate of Raseri Kanton was just as horrifying: the whole city fell into the earth.
2114394606:
Yalakheen

Yalakheen is a sacred city of the deity Yahaiya located in the Alavah Desert of Qadira. Its residents are priestesses of the goddess who tend to visitors seeking their prayers to be fulfilled, usually through suicidal sacrifice.
2120755956:
Beachcomber

The town of Beachcomber on Bag Island in the Shackles is mainly inhabited by halflings, but also a large number of liberated dromaars live in its Lurker District. The port is rowdy and shady characters are often attracted to the town.

An increasingly bold temple to Norgorber is located at the north edge of the town, and it is said that secret tunnels beneath the temple lead to the vast catacombs under the town. Despite the freedom of religion on Bag Island, most of Norgorber's faithful still hide their identity. The two priests of Norgorber in town are Antal Armskeep and Black Gnarea and they are almost open about their faith. They jointly command the ketch Darkbow. The priest-captains are brutally efficient at hunting and destroying slave ships, and often sacrifice the captured slavers to Norgorber.

2133374696:
Ironworks

The Ironworks is one of the largest buildings in Old Korvosa and indeed the entire city. The Ironworks gets pig iron from Janderhoff and recycled slag from local sources and churns out wrought iron of a variety of qualities. Much more a factory than a conventional smithy, the Ironworks focuses on quantity over quality and so never clashes with the city's more quality-focused blacksmiths.

Many of the employees at the Ironworks are also members of the secret guild called the Ironsoots.
